prompt: A construction worker prying up a stone with a wooden board, lever principle, fulcrum, lever arm, and heavy object clearly labeled.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a person whose appearance presents as a construction worker in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Unrelated to current world knowledge questions, confirming the existence of the person entity. PASS when a person with obvious construction worker characteristics (such as wearing work clothes, a hard hat, etc.) appears in the image, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a long wooden board in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Unrelated to current world knowledge questions, confirming the existence of the prop entity. PASS when a long wooden board or similar board material is present in the image, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a stone in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure visual judgment. Unrelated to current world knowledge questions, confirming the existence of the target heavy object entity. PASS when an obvious stone is present in the image, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: There is a smaller object placed on the ground under the wooden board (acting as a fulcrum) in the image.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit knowledge recall verification. The lever principle strictly requires a fulcrum. PASS when a smaller volume object (such as a small stone, wooden stake, brick, etc.) is placed on the ground underneath the wooden board as a support, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: The wooden board is placed on a smaller support, and one end of the board is inserted under the stone or positioned close to the bottom of the stone.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit knowledge recall verification (lever structure combination). PASS when the long wooden board rests on the smaller base support, and one end of the board is inserted under the large stone or in close contact with the bottom of the large stone, forming a mechanical structure ready to pry; FAIL when the wooden board is laid flat, has no physical interaction with the small/large stone, or the structure does not conform to the seesaw principle.

vqa_checklist.question: The construction worker is applying downward pressure or action to the end of the wooden board away from the stone.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit physical knowledge verification (effort application). The worker must apply pressure to the end of the wooden board away from the stone to generate the torque to lift the stone. PASS when the worker is positioned at the side of the board not touching the stone and performs force-applying actions such as pressing down or stepping; FAIL when the worker's action does not interact with the wooden board, or pulls the board upward without fulcrum constraints.

vqa_checklist.question: The image contains the text characters for "fulcrum" or its equivalent English words.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. Tests the model's ability to render specific text. PASS when the image contains the text characters for "支点" or its equivalent English texts like "Fulcrum" or "Pivot"; FAIL if there is a spelling error or the text does not exist.

vqa_checklist.question: The text "fulcrum" clearly indicates the support object under the wooden board.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Knowledge annotation verification. Tests the model's understanding of the physical definition of "fulcrum" in the lever principle. PASS when the "fulcrum" text points via an arrow, a connecting line, or is directly placed next to the small object acting as a support under the wooden board; FAIL if it points to the worker, the large stone, or the effort end of the wooden board.

vqa_checklist.question: The image contains the text characters for "heavy object" or its equivalent English words.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. Tests the model's ability to render specific text. PASS when the image contains the text characters for "重物" or its equivalent English texts like "Load", "Weight", or "Resistance"; FAIL if there is a spelling error or the text does not exist.

vqa_checklist.question: The text "heavy object" clearly indicates the large stone being pried.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Knowledge annotation verification. Tests the model's understanding of the physical definition of "heavy object (source of load/resistance)" in the lever principle. PASS when the "heavy object" text points via an arrow, a connecting line, or is directly placed next to the large stone being pried; FAIL if it points to the worker or the support object.

vqa_checklist.question: The image contains the text characters for "lever arm", related specific distinctions (such as effort arm/load arm), or equivalent English words.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. Tests the model's ability to render specific text. PASS when the image contains the text characters "力臂", "动力臂", "阻力臂" or equivalent English texts like "Lever arm", "Moment arm", "Effort arm", or "Load arm"; FAIL if there is a spelling error or the text does not exist.

vqa_checklist.question: The image contains a graphical symbol (such as a bidirectional arrow or brace) used to indicate a length or distance range.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. Visual auxiliary graphic presentation. The annotation of a lever arm requires not only text but also graphics indicating the range. PASS when auxiliary graphics such as line segments, bidirectional arrows, or braces indicating a length interval appear in the image, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: The text annotation for "lever arm" is closely combined and used as a set with the graphical symbol indicating the length range.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Pure instruction following. Text and graphics should be logically and typographically bound. PASS when the "lever arm" text and the graphical symbol indicating the length are spatially close to each other and clearly serve as the same annotation combination, otherwise FAIL.

vqa_checklist.question: The two ends of the graphical symbol indicating the length of the lever arm respectively mark the distance between the fulcrum position and the position of the effort/load application point.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Implicit knowledge verification. Tests the physical definition of the lever arm. The lever arm is the perpendicular distance from the fulcrum to the line of action of the force. In simple diagrams, it is usually represented as a line segment along the wooden board from the fulcrum position to the point of action of the effort (worker's hands) or load (large stone). PASS when one end of the length indicating graphic aligns with the support (fulcrum) and the other end aligns with the worker's effort point or the large stone; FAIL if the indicating graphic fails to use the fulcrum as one end baseline or spans an incorrect spatial distance.

vqa_checklist.question: There are unidirectional arrows indicating the direction of the applied forces in the image, and the direction of the arrows conforms to the actual force rules under the current lever state (e.g., gravity/pressure downward, support force upward).
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. Analysis of forces that easily goes out of bounds in physical diagrams. If there are no unidirectional arrows for force directions in the image, silently NA; if the model actively generates arrows indicating force directions, PASS when the load/resistance arrow at the large stone points downward, the effort arrow at the worker points downward, and the support force arrow at the fulcrum points upward; FAIL when the direction of the arrows violates the actual physical laws of forces in this scenario.

vqa_checklist.question: There are extra annotations containing the text "lever principle" or related mathematical formulas for torque equilibrium (such as F1×L1 = F2×L2) in the image, and the content is correct without errors.
vqa_checklist.knowledge_hint: Optional knowledge annotation task. The prompt contains the words "lever principle", which the model might render as a title in the image. If texts like "杠杆原理" or "Lever Principle" do not exist in the image, silently NA; if the model actively generates this text, PASS as long as it is spelled correctly without scientific errors; FAIL if the text contains a lot of gibberish spelling or incorrect formulas (such as F1/L1=F2/L2).

cot_prompt: A construction site scene. In the center of the image, there is a massive stone. On the ground next to the large stone, a small stone or a section of log is placed. A thick, long wooden board rests on the small stone, with one end tilting downward and wedged under the bottom of the large stone. A construction worker wearing work clothes and a safety helmet stands at the other upward-tilted end of the wooden board, forcefully pressing down on the board with both hands or his body. The image contains clear text annotations and graphic symbols: next to the small stone, the text "fulcrum" is written, pointing to the small stone via a connecting line or layout arrangement; next to the large stone, the text "load" is written, pointing to the large stone via a connecting line or layout arrangement; on one side of the wooden board, a graphic symbol indicating a length range is drawn (such as a curly brace or a line segment with limit marks at both ends), with one end of the line segment horizontally aligned with the small stone, and the other end horizontally aligned with the position of the worker's hands or the large stone, and next to this line segment, the text "lever arm" is written.
